query_normalized: predicting the diffusion coefficient of hydrogen in metals from compositional and microstructural descriptors materials science
rationale: This paper directly addresses the user's question by employing machine learning to predict hydrogen diffusion properties (specifically activation energy, which determines the diffusion coefficient) in metals and alloys using compositional and physical descriptors, satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (b) for measuring the dependent variable on the same domain.
